---
title: "References"
---

## Books

- [Salmon Fever, River's End](https://amatobooks.com/products/salmon-fever-rivers-end-tragedies-on-the-lower-columbia-river-in-the-1870s-1880s-and-1890s-by-liisa-penner)
- [Chinook Story](https://chinookstory.org/)
- [World's Most Dangerous]()
- [Pacific Graveyard]()
- [Graveyard of the Pacific]()
- [Chinookan Peoples of the Lower Columbia]()

## Articles

- [In 1880s, salmon were the “most dangerous catch](https://www.offbeatoregon.com/1206d-most-dangerous-catch-salmon-on-columbia-river-bar.html)
- [Hanthorn Cannery Museum](https://www.orartswatch.org/can-do-astorias-hanthorn-cannery-museum/)
- [This Nest of Dangers May 1880: How legends come to be](https://chinookobserver.com/2018/03/29/this-nest-of-dangers-may-1880-how-legends-come-to-be/#google_vignette)

## Websites

- [Hanthorn Cannery Museum](https://canneryworker.org/history/)
- [Chinook Nation](https://chinooknation.org/)
